idea.hook: Think space travel is easy? Try reaching our NEAREST exoplanet.
facts: Destination: Proxima Centauri b, 4.2 Light Years away.
At current fastest probe speed (~200 km/s, Parker Solar Probe): ~6,300 YEARS.
At hypothetical 'Generation Ship' speed (0.01c or 1% light speed): ~420 YEARS.
At Light Speed (c) – The Cosmic Limit: 4.2 YEARS.
idea.payoff: Even our closest star requires centuries. Interstellar travel redefines 'long journey'!
idea.title: How long to reach the NEAREST Alien World? 🚀

scenes.visual: Earth slowly receding into a vast, dark starfield. A question mark subtly pulses.

scenes.text:
Destination: Proxima Centauri b
4.2 Light Years Away
scenes.visual: A distant red dwarf star (Proxima Centauri) with a small exoplanet orbiting. A subtle dotted line traces from a tiny Earth icon (bottom left) towards the star system.

scenes.text:
At fastest probe speed (~200 km/s):
~6,300 YEARS
scenes.visual: A small, modern space probe icon (e.g., Parker Solar Probe) streaks from left to right across the starfield. Text slides in from the left.
A large, detailed concept art of a futuristic generation ship slowly traverses the screen from right to left. Text slides in from the right.

scenes.text:
At Light Speed (c) – The Cosmic Limit: 4.2 YEARS

Interstellar travel: A journey of CENTURIES!
scenes.visual: A brilliant flash of light and stars streaking past, symbolizing light speed. This transitions into the distant Proxima Centauri b system, emphasizing its continued vastness.

youtube.title: How long to reach the NEAREST Alien World? 🚀
